title: product_component_selectors
description: Maps a component category + match conditions to a SKU (or SKU pattern). When the engine needs to emit a line of component_category X, it scans selectors for that category ordered by priority (lower first) and picks the first whose match_json matches the current context. sku_pattern supports placeholders: {colour}, {finish}, {frame_cap_size}, {selected_hinge_sku}, {selected_latch_sku} — resolved at BOM-run time.

selector_key.description: Short stable identifier unique within the product.

component_category.description: Must match a category value used in product_components and referenced by engine rules. Examples: 'slat', 'side_frame', 'cfc_cover', 'centre_support_rail', 'f_section', 'gate_side_frame', 'joiner_block'.

selector_type.description: Currently only 'exact' is widely used. Reserved for future strategies.

match_json.description: Key/value predicates matched against the current context (job + run + segment + derived variables). Empty {} matches always.

qty_key.description: Name of the segCtx variable that holds the quantity for this selector group. The engine groups selectors by (component_category, qty_key) and emits one line per group. Selectors without qty_key are skipped with an assumption log entry.

sku_pattern.description: Resolved SKU. Placeholders are substituted at BOM-run time. Example: 'XP-6100-S65-{colour}' → 'XP-6100-S65-B' when colour_code is 'B'.
